title: ODI Matrix — NAICS 221115 Wind Electric Power Generation

sections.title: Step 1 — Error statements gathered
sections.content:
| Source | Count | IDs |
|---|---|---|
| Step 06 market needs (P1/P2) | 21 | N1–N21 |
| Step 06 bridge needs (P3–P5) | 3 | N22, N23, N24 |
| Step 10 product-related needs (affected_markets ⊇ 221115) | 10 | PRN1–PRN10 |
| **Total scored entries** | **34** | |

Step-10 injections carry `job_step_number=0`, `job_step_name="product_constraint"`, `impact_category="reliability"`, `product_related=true`.

---

sections.title: Step 2 — Satisfaction recalibration (incumbent-anchored)
sections.content:
**Anchors:** Fluence (BESS turnkey, grid-forming ref) · Wärtsilä GEMS (mature dispatch EMS) · Tesla Megapack (product-with-install) · Siemens Gamesa Storage (wind-OEM, grid-forming ref) · utility in-house EPC (EnBW / RWE / Vattenfall).

### Recalibration deltas (only entries whose satisfaction moved)

| # | Statement (short) | Sat₀ | Sat₁ | Rationale (incumbent evidence) |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| N2 | Bankable dispatch-revenue model | 3 | 4 | Wärtsilä GEMS / Fluence Mosaic ship revenue-stack modeling; still gap vs. bankable single-study. |
| N12 | Grid-forming cert gap (2026 inertia) | 3 | 4 | Fluence + SGRE have public grid-forming reference plants; field is maturing. |
| N13 | aFRR/FCR <30 s response | 6 | 7 | Mature across incumbents; productized in GEMS/Mosaic. |
| N14 | Curtailment capture >90% | 5 | 6 | Curtailment strategies productized at utility scale (Wärtsilä, Fluence). |
| N15 | PCS FRT trip (VDE-AR-N 4110) | 6 | 7 | Fluence / SGRE reference sites pass FRT reliably. |
| N18 | Re-tune EMS effort | 4 | 5 | GEMS / Mosaic ship adaptive retune + rolling-horizon solvers. |
| PRN6 | Roundtrip-loss revenue shortfall | 5 | 6 | EMS optimizers recover part of the loss via arbitrage stacking. |
| PRN8 | TR3 grid-forming cert gap | 3 | 4 | Same rationale as N12. |
| PRN9 | Aging-aware-cycling warranty breach | 5 | 6 | Incumbent BMS firmware includes warranty-aware cycling. |

All other entries retain their Step 06 / Step 10 satisfaction scores (incumbent evidence neither strengthens nor contradicts). Importance held constant (per spec "importance stability rule").

---

sections.title: Step 3 — Opportunity scores (deterministic)
sections.content:
`opportunity_score = importance + max(importance − satisfaction, 0)`
`is_underserved = importance > 7 AND satisfaction < 5`
`is_overserved = importance < 3 AND satisfaction > 7`

### 3a — Market needs (N1–N24)

| # | Step | Statement (short) | Imp | Sat | Opp | US | OS | p_rel |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|

sections.title: Step 4 — Market ODI summary
sections.content:
| Field | Value |
|---|---|
| market_name | Wind+BESS Hybrid Parks |
| naics_code | 221115 |
| total_needs | 34 |
| underserved_count | 13 |
| overserved_count | 0 |
| avg_opportunity_score | **11.50** |
| top_5_opportunity_score | N4 (16.00) → N10 (15.00) → N1 / N6 / N9 (14.00) |

### Top 5 opportunities (by score; ties broken by ID order)

| Rank | # | Opp | Error statement |
|---|---|---|---|
| 1 | N4 | 16.00 | Minimize the likelihood of discovering a DSO MV-capacity blocker after permit milestone, causing >6-month project restart |
| 2 | N10 | 15.00 | Minimize the likelihood of commissioning holdup caused by firmware-version incompatibility between BMS, PCS and EMS subsystems |
| 3 | N1 | 14.00 | Minimize the likelihood of undersizing BESS capacity by >15% relative to curtailment-capture potential due to incomplete site wind-yield and congestion-hour data |
| 4 | N6 | 14.00 | Minimize the time-to-Ready-to-Build from contract signature to <9 months including Baugenehmigung, DSO acceptance and procurement |
| 5 | N9 | 14.00 | Minimize the likelihood of FGW TR8 plant-cert re-submission due to incomplete TR3 unit-cert handover from component vendors |

### Other 14.0 ties (runners-up, worth reading alongside top-5)

N12 (grid-forming cert gap) · PRN8 (TR3 grid-forming cert gap — product-related restatement of N12) · PRN10 (DSO protection-coord study on wind MV cabinet).

### Cluster read

| Lifecycle phase | Underserved count | Read |
|---|---|---|
| DEFINE | 2 (N1, N2) | Sales-enablement / sizing + bankable revenue model. |
| LOCATE | 1 (N4) + PRN10 | DSO/MV-side surprises dominate. |
| PREPARE | 2 (N6, N8) + PRN1 | Cert + fire-clearance drive RtB slip. |
| CONFIRM | 3 (N9, N10, N12) + PRN8 | **Largest underserved cluster — all certification / handover.** |
| CONCLUDE | 1 (N21) | End-of-life Battery-Reg reporting. |
| Emotional anchor | N24 | Single-counterparty fault-wrap. |

DEFINE + LOCATE + PREPARE + CONFIRM = **10 of 13 underserved entries** concentrated in pre-operation phases. Post-operation (EXECUTE / MONITOR / MODIFY / CONCLUDE) is appropriately served or mildly underserved.

---

sections.title: Step 5 — Cross-check vs. Step 09 Feature-Market-Fit
sections.content:
| Metric | Value | Threshold | Trigger |
|---|---|---|---|
| ODI avg opportunity | 11.50 | > 12 | no |
| FMF avg (tech features) | 6.11 | < 4 | no |
| ODI avg | 11.50 | < 8 | no |
| FMF avg | 6.11 | > 7 | no |

**Cross-check result: no inconsistencies logged.**

Feature-level read (informal, not a QA gate):
- **Grid-Code Certification Delivery (F5, FMF=7.78)** maps to N6 / N9 / N11 / N12 / N20 / PRN8 — 4 of the top-5 underserved CONFIRM-cluster needs. Feature fit and ODI opportunity both point to cert delivery as the leverage point.
- **EMS (F4, FMF=6.56)** maps to N2 / N14 / N18 — all appropriately-served post-recalibration. Mein Solar's V4 inferior position (vs. Wärtsilä GEMS) is visible but the needs themselves are not underserved — suggesting the EMS gap is a *vendor-vs-incumbent* gap, not a *market-unmet* gap.
- **Containerized BESS (F1) / MV Coupling (F3) / Civil Works (F8)** all FMF 5.33–5.78, match ODI `appropriately served` entries (N5 / N7 / N16 / N17 / PRN3 / PRN5) — commodity baseline confirmed from both angles.

---

sections.title: Step 6 — Neo4j write preview (Step 12 persistence)
sections.content:
```
MERGE (m:Market {naics_code:"221115", run_id})
  SET  m.odi_total_needs = 34,
       m.odi_underserved_count = 13,
       m.odi_overserved_count = 0,
       m.odi_avg_opportunity_score = 11.50,
       m.odi_top_opportunities = "[\"...\", ...]"  // JSON-encoded top-5

FOREACH entry IN entries:
  MERGE (os:OpportunityScore {
    error_statement: substring(full_statement, 0, 80),
    naics_code: "221115",
    run_id
  })
    SET  os.full_statement, os.market_name, os.importance,
         os.satisfaction_current, os.opportunity_score,
         os.is_underserved, os.is_overserved, os.product_related
  MERGE (m)-[:has_opportunity_score]->(os)
```

---

sections.title: QA checklist
sections.content:
| # | Check | Status | Note |
|---|---|---|---|
| Q1 | Formula correct (spot-check 5) | **PASS** | N4 10+6=16 · N10 9+6=15 · N1 9+5=14 · N6 9+5=14 · N17 6+1=7. All match. |
| Q2 | Underserved flags correct | **PASS** | All 13 entries satisfy imp>7 AND sat<5; no false positives (N3 imp=7 excluded; N5/N11 sat=5 excluded). |
| Q3 | Overserved flags correct | **PASS** | 0 entries satisfy imp<3 AND sat>7; 0 flagged. |
| Q4 | Need-count preservation | **PASS** | 24 Step-06 needs retained + 10 Step-10 additions = 34 ≥ 24. |
| Q5 | Top-5 ordering | **PASS** | Descending by opp; ties broken by ID order (N1<N6<N9<N12). |
| Q6 | Cross-check ran | **PASS** | ODI vs FMF computed; no threshold triggered. |

---

sections.title: Strategic implications
sections.content:
| Signal | Implication |
|---|---|
| 10 of 13 underserved entries sit pre-operation (DEFINE → CONFIRM) | Mein Solar's EPC wrap is an **upstream-of-revenue** product; the sales promise is schedule + certification certainty, not operating efficiency. |
| CONFIRM cluster (N9, N10, N12, PRN8) — 4 underserved entries, highest density | Certification + firmware-handover is the **productizable differentiator**. Own TR3 + TR8 dossier; own BMS/PCS/EMS integration-validation. |
| N4 opp=16 (DSO MV-capacity blocker) + PRN10 opp=14 (protection-coord study) | Pre-contract DSO-feasibility study is a **billable de-risking service**, not a free pre-sale. Package it as Phase 0. |
| N24 opp=13 (single-counterparty fault-wrap) + Step 09 V3 `superior` | Emotional anchor is validated by ODI — sell the warranty-wrap, not the equipment. |
| PRN8 opp=14 (grid-forming TR3 cert) time-boxed Jan 2026 | Reference-plant commitment in 2026 H1 is the highest-opportunity time-boxed bet. |
| PRN1 opp=12 (Feuerwehr redesign) | Region-indexed fire-clearance template library → priced delta on EPC contract. |

---

rows.Rationale (incumbent evidence): Wärtsilä GEMS / Fluence Mosaic ship revenue-stack modeling; still gap vs. bankable single-study.
Fluence + SGRE have public grid-forming reference plants; field is maturing.
Mature across incumbents; productized in GEMS/Mosaic.
Curtailment strategies productized at utility scale (Wärtsilä, Fluence).
Fluence / SGRE reference sites pass FRT reliably.
GEMS / Mosaic ship adaptive retune + rolling-horizon solvers.
EMS optimizers recover part of the loss via arbitrage stacking.
Incumbent BMS firmware includes warranty-aware cycling.

rows.Statement (short): DSO protection-coord study on wind MV cabinet

rows.Signal: N4 opp=16 (DSO MV-capacity blocker) + PRN10 opp=14 (protection-coord study)
rows.Implication: Pre-contract DSO-feasibility study is a **billable de-risking service**, not a free pre-sale. Package it as Phase 0.

rows.Implication: Reference-plant commitment in 2026 H1 is the highest-opportunity time-boxed bet.
Region-indexed fire-clearance template library → priced delta on EPC contract.

weaknesses: splits PV + BESS scope into separate GCs and TR8 dossiers

entries.error_statement: Minimize the likelihood of undersizing BESS capacity by >15% relative to curtailment-capture potential due to incomplete site wind-yield and congestion-hour data
Increase the ability to produce a bankable dispatch-and-revenue model linking curtailment, §19 StromNEV, intraday arbitrage, FCR/aFRR and inertia within a single sizing study
Minimize the likelihood of missing the inertia-service revenue track (EUR 8-20 k per MW per year from Jan 2026) during initial business-case definition
Minimize the likelihood of discovering a DSO MV-capacity blocker after permit milestone, causing >6-month project restart
Increase the ability to secure a buildable 40-80 m2 per MWh footprint inside existing wind-park fencing without new land lease or Bebauungsplan change
Minimize the time-to-Ready-to-Build from contract signature to under 9 months including Baugenehmigung, DSO acceptance and procurement
Minimize variability between planned and as-built BoP scope (cable runs, foundations, Feuerwehr access) causing >5% cost overrun
Minimize the likelihood of schedule slip driven by Feuerwehr/VdS fire-protection clearance re-design after permit submission
Minimize the likelihood of FGW TR8 plant-cert re-submission due to incomplete TR3 unit-cert handover from component vendors
Minimize the likelihood of commissioning holdup caused by firmware-version incompatibility between BMS, PCS and EMS subsystems
Minimize the time to close VDE-AR-N 4110/4120 witness-test non-conformities to under 30 days from finding
Minimize the likelihood of grid-forming-mode certification gaps that disqualify the asset from the 2026 inertia-service product
Minimize the likelihood of missing an aFRR or FCR activation command within the required response window (<30 s)
Maximize the share of available curtailment-window MWh captured by the BESS to above 90% of DSO-curtailed energy
Minimize the likelihood of PCS trip under grid-fault ride-through conditions specified by VDE-AR-N 4110
Minimize the likelihood of undetected capacity fade exceeding warranty threshold prior to annual availability audit
Minimize SoC-reporting drift between BMS, EMS and aggregator to under 1% over a 24-hour cycle
Minimize the operator effort to re-tune EMS dispatch policy when curtailment patterns or intraday spreads shift materially
Minimize the likelihood that aging-aware cycling misconfiguration drives degradation exceeding contractual throughput guarantee
Minimize the effort to execute annual FGW/DSO re-certification audits through a single pre-packaged evidence dossier
Minimize the likelihood of end-of-life disposal non-compliance with EU Battery Regulation reporting obligations
Increase the ability to present the BESS addition as a revenue-accretive asset with committed multi-stream dispatch evidence at investment-committee
Minimize the likelihood of certifier audit findings that require repeat visits or signal sloppy engineering practice
Minimize the time lost to cross-vendor root-cause dispute by ensuring a single EPC counterparty owns fault-tree triage and warranty-wrap resolution

Minimize the likelihood of schedule slip driven by Feuerwehr / VdS fire-clearance redesign triggered by Li-ion deflagration-venting and compartmentation rules that do not apply to non-flammable storage chemistries
Minimize the likelihood of insurance-premium step-up or coverage denial driven by Li-ion fire-loss loss-history that flow/flywheel/CAES assets avoid

Increase the ability to fit the storage asset inside an existing wind-park fenced footprint at 40-80 m2/MWh without a new Bebauungsplan change - a footprint a flywheel-farm or standalone condenser would not require
Minimize the likelihood of procurement disruption from LFP-cell CN-supplier concentration, FX swings, CRMA / EU Battery Regulation change - exposure absent for mechanical and thermal storage
Minimize the likelihood of derating or cycle-life loss when exposed ambient drifts below -20 C or above +50 C - a constraint flow-battery stacks and CAES tolerate differently
Minimize the likelihood of a revenue shortfall from 10-15% roundtrip-loss floor eating intraday-arbitrage and curtailment-capture margin - worse than pumped-hydro at comparable scale
Minimize the likelihood of business-case failure when installed cost exceeds EUR 250-400 per kWh - a price envelope a thermal-storage or hybrid-H2 option clears differently

Minimize the likelihood that FGW TR8 + TR3 grid-forming-mode certification gaps disqualify the asset from the Jan-2026 TSO inertia-service product - a mechanism-specific gap a synchronous condenser would not face

Minimize the likelihood of warranty-throughput breach driven by aging-aware-cycling misconfiguration on LFP cells - a failure mode electromechanical stores (flywheel) and mechanical stores (CAES) do not share

Minimize the likelihood of DSO rejection when the BESS is added to a pre-energized wind MV cabinet without revised protection-coordination study - driven by BESS bidirectional PCS behaviour alien to generator-only wind connections
